title: Local Revenue Sources: Bond Issues
description: Conroe ISD follows specific rules when asking voters to approve bonds, which are a common way school districts raise money for major expenses like new buildings, renovations, and equipment. The policy outlines the legal steps the district must take before placing a bond measure on the ballot and how those funds can be used once approved. Community members who vote in bond elections are directly impacted, since approved bonds are typically repaid through local property taxes.
